title: Architecture
description: The Architect Reviewer's structured output for a feature, persisted as features/<F>/architecture.json (the machine-checkable companion to architecture.md). NFRs live HERE, not on the spec-author-owned, spec-gated feature-spec.json/story.json: the architect proposes them and the HIL (Product Owner) accepts or modifies them at Gate 2. Moving NFRs off feature-spec.json resolves the spec-gate integrity drift: the architect annotating feature-spec.json after the spec gate hashed it tripped verify-gate-integrity.

service_backed.description: True when the feature persists data or carries business logic (anything beyond a trivial static/read-through endpoint). A service-backed feature MUST declare layered `layers` and be defended by a layering fitness test; checkLayeringDeclared hard-blocks Gate 2 when it is service_backed with no boundary+service+repository. Omit/false for a trivial feature (the YAGNI guard, so layering is not forced where it is not warranted).

layers.description: The module layering the build must realize (boundary -> service -> repository -> ORM, dependencies inward). Required in substance for a service_backed feature. The boundary never imports the DB session; persistence lives only in the repository; business logic only in the service.

module.description: Path/dotted-module this layer lives in (e.g. app/routes, app/services, app/repositories).

may_import.description: Layer roles this one is allowed to depend on (inward). Empty/omitted means it depends on nothing inward.

nfrs.description: Non-functional requirements proposed by the architect, adjudicated by the HIL at Gate 2. An empty array is valid (a feature may have no NFRs), but every entry that exists must be HIL-considered, not unconsidered.

applies_to.description: Feature or story id this NFR scopes to. Omit for feature-wide NFRs.

hil_status.description: Architect proposes; the HIL accepts/modifies/rejects at Gate 2.

brief_ref.description: The R<n> id of the nfrs.md Required item this NFR satisfies. Every Required item in the HIL's nfrs.md must be covered by some architecture.json nfr carrying its id here, or checkNfrCoverage hard-blocks the architecture gate. Omit for architect-originated NFRs that no Required item drove.
